message:
feat(python): polyadd / polysub / polymul wrapper parity

Adds three passthrough wrappers for numpy's deprecated-but-still-used
old-polynomial-API arithmetic helpers:

- polyadd(a1, a2) — left-zero-pads the shorter input so like-degree
  terms align before summing; length is max(len(a1), len(a2)).
- polysub(a1, a2) — same alignment rule, returns a1 - a2.
- polymul(a1, a2) — 1-D only; output length len(a1) + len(a2) - 1.

A single live-NumPy parity test exercises all three across seven cases
per op (equal length, length-mismatched in both directions, empty left,
empty right, scalar × scalar, and the docs' degree-2 × degree-1 case),
plus a 2-D input rejection check for polymul's "Polynomial must be 1d
only." ValueError path.

Complements the earlier np.polyder (franken_numpy-aujd) and
np.polyint wrappers; closes the top-level np.poly* arithmetic gap
except for polydiv / polyfit / poly / poly1d which return non-array
shapes and warrant separate wrappers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
